summary: Gruber responds to Tim Bajarin's argument that Siri threatens Google and Microsoft's search businesses. While agreeing Siri is a huge deal that could disrupt traditional web search, Gruber rejects Bajarin's suggestion that Apple should monetize Siri through search advertising, calling it 'tacky.' Instead, Gruber argues Apple's business model is fundamentally different: Siri exists to sell more devices, not to generate ad revenue. He traces an evolution of tech business models from Microsoft's software licensing to Google's ad-supported free services to Apple's premium device model. Gruber sees iCloud as the template — a free, ad-free service that exists purely to enhance the device ownership experience.
key_points: Siri represents a potential long-term threat to Google and Microsoft's search businesses, but calling search a Microsoft 'core business' is a stretch given their consistent losses in online services
Apple monetizing Siri through advertising would be fundamentally misaligned with Apple's business model and brand — Gruber dismisses it as 'tacky'
Three successive tech business models: Microsoft's pay-for-licenses, Google's ad-supported free services, and Apple's premium-device-with-free-services model
Siri's real value to Apple is as a feature that sells more devices and could become the interface for entirely new product categories
iCloud exemplifies Apple's approach: absorbing service costs as part of the device ownership experience rather than monetizing through ads
money_quotes: I can't see Apple monetizing it through advertising. That's tacky.

Google's ad-driven model disrupted Microsoft's pay-for-software-licenses model. Apple's just-buy-our-devices-and-look-at-all-the-cool-shit-you-get-with-them model could disrupt Google's ad-driven model.

Microsoft's model was: you'd buy a device, then pay for licenses for Microsoft software. Google's disruption was: hey, you don't need to pay for Microsoft software if you're willing to put up with our non-blinking mostly-text ads. Apple's model is: you don't even need to see those ads, just buy your devices from us.
Siri doesn't need to lead to advertising in order to add to Apple's bottom line.
Apple now offers free-of-charge online services ad-free. It's a sunk cost in the name of the overall experience for Apple device buyers.
Siri could be the interface to future products, like tiny little Nano-sized devices, or home entertainment systems.
key_insight: Apple's business model disrupts Google's ad-driven model by bundling services like Siri as device-selling features rather than monetizing them through advertising.
